item.title: What fasting for Ramadan is like for a sailor, a midwife and a miner
item.description: Australians in high-stakes workplaces — from ships to hospitals to mine sites — reveal the realities of observing Ramadan while on the job.

item.title: Israel's approval of West Bank land registration condemned as 'de facto annexation'
item.description: The land registration adds to a series of measures taken earlier this ⁠month to expand control.

item.title: 'Not fair dinkum': ACCC rejects Coles' arguments in landmark 'illusory' discounts legal case
item.description: The consumer watchdog's barrister said increased costs are irrelevant to whether products are promoted to customers as genuine discounts.

item.title: 'Iran is my home as well': Worldwide turnout of hundreds of thousands in solidarity with Iranian protesters
item.description: From Munich to Melbourne, hundreds of thousands have turned out to oppose Iran's clerical leadership in the wake of a brutal crackdown on protesters inside the country. While opinions differ over who should take over the current regime, protesters all voiced their desire for an Iran free from oppression.

item.title: Court appearance for alleged Bondi shooter | SBS News In Easy English 16 February 2026
item.description: A daily 5-minute news bulletin for English learners and people with a disability.

item.title: Opposition was preparing widespread immigration bans under Sussan Ley's leadership
item.description: The Opposition's immigration spokesperson under Ley has said he never proposed nor agreed to the policy.

item.title: Palestinians look to salvage Gaza's history from the ruins of war
item.description: .The Gaza Strip is home to some extraordinary remnants of human history, dating from the Bronze Age through to the Ottoman and British empires.Many historic sites suffered from neglect before the war - but the United Nations cultural agency, UNESCO, says it has verified damage to at least 150 historic heritage sites since the start of the Israel-Hamas conflict. Palestinians in the Gaza Strip are reckoning with how to save their historical monuments.

item.title: Tech stocks recover and IMF warns Australian states on spending
item.description: SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Alex Pikoulas from Munjarra Capital about the day's market action including the latest profit results and the turn around in the tech sector; plus Anthony Walker from SP Global Ratings after the IMF warned Australian states to reign in spending.

item.title: Kath hosted her late partner Nigel's wake while he was still alive
item.description: Living funerals are an emerging practice in Australia, allowing people to say goodbye to loved ones before they die.

item.title: 'Very rare': Israeli prosecutors to charge West Bank settler over killing of Palestinian activist
item.description: The decision is an uncommon legal step amid mounting criticism over accountability for violence in the occupied territories.

item.title: Who are the Australians with alleged IS links seeking to return from a Syrian camp?
item.description: The government says it won't help repatriate a group of Australian women and children with links to the self-proclaimed Islamic State group.

item.title: How the tale of one possum reveals threats to thousands of Aussie species
item.description: Australia’s unique wildlife is facing an escalating extinction crisis, driven primarily by climate change and compounded by habitat loss. 34 new species of plants and animals have been added to the government's threatened species list this February, including the lemuroid ringtail possum - native to north Queensland's Wet Tropics region. Researchers say urgent climate action and stronger environmental protections are critical to prevent further irreversible loss of Australia's signature biodiversity.

item.title: PM rules out repatriation for Australians with alleged IS links in Syria | SBS News In Easy English 17 February 2026
item.description: A daily 5-minute news bulletin for English learners and people with a disability.

item.title: BHP CFO on copper growth opportunties, iron ore future and growing dividends
item.description: SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with BHP CFO Vandita Pant following the miner's US$5.6bn half-year profit to find out more about its growing copper business and future dividend policy; plus Jonathan Shead from State Street Investment Management on the investment environment.

item.title: 'It was a no-brainer': The baby boomer retirement myth being upended
item.description: You might think it's boomers and empty nesters moving to these parts of Australia, according to an expert. New data tells a different story.

item.title: Sabina quit snacking after monitoring her sugar levels. Is the data that simple?
item.description: Continuous glucose monitors provide live data for blood sugar levels. But is access to this health data a good thing for non-diabetics?

item.title: Government stands firm on not assisting or repatriating IS families in Syria
item.description: The last remaining Australian women and children with links to the IS Group have been sent back to a Syrian camp after attempting to repatriate themselves home. The group of 34 left al-Roj in the country's northeast overnight - but a communications issue with Damascus forced their return to the camp.

item.title: Tilda Swinton, Javier Bardem and others criticise Berlin Film Festival's 'silence' on Gaza
item.description: An open letter signed by film industry figures condemned the Berlin Film Festival's "neutral stance" on Gaza.

item.title: Man with shot gun arrested in Capitol Hill | SBS News in Easy English 18 February 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English Language learners

item.title: Government bans woman with alleged IS links from returning to Australia
item.description: Human rights advocates say the move could render the Australian "stateless", stressing the government's duty under international law.

item.title: No, not even water: A guide to Ramadan for non-Muslims
item.description: Wonder what Ramadan is all about? Here's why many Muslims fast during the holy month, and how to best support your practising friends.

item.title: No, not even water: A guide to Ramadan for non-Muslims
item.description: Wonder what Ramadan is all about? Here's why many Muslims fast during the holy month, and how to best support your practising friends.

item.title: Muslim leaders' three requests as Australia's largest mosque receives new threatening letter
item.description: It comes amid unease and safety concerns in Muslim communities across the country ahead of Ramadan.

item.title: Inflation eats into wages as cost of living worsens
item.description: SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Devika Shivadear from RSM Australia about the latest Wage Price Index which shows real wages has gone backwards; plus a look at the day on the sharemarket with Jun Bei Lui from Ten Cap.

item.title: The super mistake that could cost you more than $120,000 at retirement
item.description: One in four Australians couldn't answer a key question about their super, a new survey found, prompting a warning from a peak body.

item.title: Eight skiers found dead after California avalanche, one still missing
item.description: Rescuers had earlier reached six survivors in "extreme" conditions near Lake Tahoe.

item.title: How Palestinians in Gaza are marking their first Ramadan since fragile truce took hold
item.description: Amid the devastation, some in Gaza City are working to revive Ramadan festivities as Palestinian Muslims celebrate the holy month.

item.title: 'Outrageous decision': Why are Ukrainian officials boycotting the Paralympics?
item.description: Paralympians from Russia and Belarus have been given the green light to compete — and not as neutral individuals.

item.title: Eight skiers die California avalanche |SBS News in Easy English 19 February 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Is Australia facing an economic reality not seen since World War Two?
item.description: As debt climbs, some economists warn Australia may face difficult choices.

item.title: Latest labour data increases chance of a rate rise, experts warn
item.description: A historically strong jobs market has raised the chances of another rate rise in 2026.

item.title: Is the US approach to Europe softening? Israel moves in the West Bank and, well, cocaine sharks
item.description: Marco Rubio sends mixed messages to Europe, Israel starts registration of large areas of the occupied West Bank and how new is Bangladesh’s new government? Plus, Chinoys celebrate lunar new year in the Philippines and Cocaine Sharks coming to waters near you.

item.title: Government blocks return of Australian woman from Syrian refugee camp for IS Group families
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke has blocked the immediate return of an Australian woman from Syria via a temporary exclusion order. It follows what the government is calling its no assistance policy for 34 citizens recently turned back by Syrian authorities. While the Prime Minister insists those who joined the IS Group must live with their choices, legal and human rights experts argue that abandoning children and coerced women in detention camps poses a greater long-term radicalisation risk than bringing them home to Australia.

item.title: Jobless rate stays at 4.1% and ASX hits another record
item.description: SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Hugh Lam, CFA from Betashares for his take on the markets as the ASX200 hits another record high, the jobless rate remains at 4.1% and takes a look at the latest company profit reports.

item.title: Calls for major change after deadly mental health escapes in Sydney
item.description: A WARNING THAT THIS STORY MAY BE DISTRESSING FOR SOME LISTENERS The New South Wales government is investigating the circumstances surrounding the escape of two patients from the same hospital, who days later were allegedly involved in separate incidents where three people died. Mental health professionals say it's again thrown the state's mental health care crisis into the spotlight.

item.title: 'Canary in the coal mine': The possum on the frontline of Australia's extinction crisis
item.description: The lemuroid ringtail possum is one of 34 species that have just been added to Australia's threatened species list.

item.title: 'Hallmarks of genocide' found in Sudan, UN says
item.description: A UN fact-finding mission concluded that the RSF paramilitary group had acted "with genocidal intent" during the seizure of Al-Fasher.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 20 February 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Trump's 'Board of Peace' flags billions in Gaza aid, US issues fresh threats against Iran
item.description: United States President Donald Trump has held the inaugural meeting of his so-called "Board of Peace", pledging AU$14.2 billion for the reconstruction of Gaza. Boasting his role as a peacemaker while leveling fresh threats against Iran, Donald Trump says the board will help achieve peace in the Middle East.

item.title: UN says siege at el-Fasher shows evidence of genocide
item.description: A United Nations fact-finding mission in Sudan has found the 18-month long siege and subsequent capture of El-Fasher in the Darfur region, last October, bears evidence of genocide. The United States, in response, has imposed sanctions on paramilitary commanders. But experts are calling for far stronger global response, to stop the violence from continuing.

item.title: US goods deficit widens despite tariffs and market reacts to QBE and Rio results
item.description: SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Marcus Bogdan from Blackmore Capital on the day's market action, including a wrap of the major profits and a look at the latest US trade figures.

item.title: Dreams of being a ballerina: Hopes of Australian child in Syrian camp for IS-linked families
item.description: An Australian child in a camp for families with links to IS fighters says she wants to come to Australia, a family member told SBS.

item.title: Is there a 'sweet spot'? These five charts are key to the migration debate
item.description: The government's opponents have fuelled debate over Australia's migration numbers.

item.title: Cafes and restaurants are closing in record numbers. This couple is bucking the trend
item.description: Australian restaurants and cafes are in crisis, according to a new report. Rising costs and falling revenue have forced one in ten food service outlets to close over the past year. However, one Australian couple has found ways to survive and thrive.

item.title: It scares me all the time': Exploring new ways to improve water safety
item.description: Australian and Indian drowning prevention experts have come together for a two-day workshop to explore new ways to reduce drowning deaths in both countries. Drowning causes tens of thousands of deaths a year in India, and in Australia, experts say the Indian diaspora is among groups that are worryingly over-represented in the country's drowning fatalities. Through exchanging ideas and building stronger cultural knowledge, workshop participants have explored new, sustainable ways to improve water safety.

item.title: INTERVIEW: New project explores power of language and shared histories
item.description: The Acknowledgement of Country has been translated into 40 languages as part of a project led by Dr Elaine Laforteza. She explains how it started and the vision for what comes next.

item.title: INTERVIEW: How students are combating racism at Australian universities
item.description: The University of Melbourne Student Union is preparing to release the findings of its fourth report on racism on campus. The union's president, Lushy Chinganya, explains what it found and how it connects with the latest Human Rights Commission report on the same issue.

item.title: 'We nearly gave up': These pizza chefs bought their first home. It hasn't been easy
item.description: Australian restaurants and cafes are in crisis, according to a new report. One Melbourne couple has found ways to survive and thrive.

item.title: 'Green light to violence': Anti-racism commissioner denounces Hanson's remarks
item.description: Australia's race discrimination commissioner says he was deeply concerned by Hanson's comments that there were "no good Muslims".

item.title: 'I knocked on every single farm': How Sammy Jeon went from backpacker to restaurateur
item.description: Renowned Korean Australian chef Sammy Jeon's culinary journey started when one regional winery took a chance on him.

item.title: Experts say Russia's government ensures its longevity by crushing dissent
item.description: This week will mark four years since Russia began its full scale invasion of Ukraine. Since then, experts say President Vladimir Putin has intensified efforts in his ongoing campaign to quash dissent and political opposition - many resistance leaders and activists inside Russia, jailed - or dead.

item.title: Australia's underground hardcore scene just went global — you can thank a flute for that
item.description: Australian hardcore band SPEED started out playing for a room of 60 people. Now they're fronting thousands.

item.title: 'What's fair?': The wages problem that's led to some roles being 'massively overpaid'
item.description: Some occupations are very well paid, and here's why.

item.title: Furious Iranian students hold anti-government protests for second day as US threats loom
item.description: The anti-government protests have led to more clashes at several Iranian universities.

item.title: 'Unintended consequences': Coalition's 'terrorist hotspot' proposal raises questions
item.description: An element of the plan is "not really known to Australian law", a legal expert says.

item.title: 'Unintended consequences': Coalition's 'terrorist hotspot' proposal raises questions
item.description: An element of the plan is "not really known to Australian law", a legal expert says.

item.title: As rate hikes loom, Australians scramble to take on increasing amounts of debt
item.description: Instead of tightening their belts, Australians are taking on more and bigger home loans, while demand for credit cards is soaring.

item.title: Ukrainians mark four years of 'pain, despair and disbelief' as Russian invasion continues
item.description: Four years after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the war shows no sign of abating, with mounting casualties, continued strikes on civilian infrastructure and faltering ceasefire efforts. As global tributes mark the February 24 anniversary, calls are growing for stronger international action to apply pressure to Russia and bring an end to Europe’s deadliest conflict since World War II.

item.title: Man shot dead attempting to enter Trump estate | SBS News in Easy English 23 February 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Australia faces drenching as storm system set to bring heavy rain and flash floods
item.description: Heavy rain and severe storms are expected across multiple states, with flash flooding, damaging winds and travel disruptions possible.

item.title: 'Abhorrent': Players targeted with racist abuse after Premier League games
item.description: Just days earlier, an investigation was opened into a Real Madrid player's claim he was racially abused by another player.

item.title: Travelling to the UK? Some dual nationals say passport changes have thrown their plans into chaos
item.description: Travel experts have raised concerns about new border rules for Australian travellers who hold dual British or Irish nationality. From February 25, dual citizens who can't present a valid British or Irish passport are at risk of being refused entry to the UK. The new regulations are causing widespread confusion, with many not even aware of their links to the country.

item.title: Are loopholes undermining Australia's support for Ukraine?
item.description: Australia has slapped nearly 2,000 sanctions on Russia — but some have questioned the effectiveness.

item.title: Trump Tower planned for Gold Coast would be Australia's tallest building, developer says
item.description: The skyscraper will reportedly be 91 storeys high and contain a six star resort, but the proposal is far from a done deal.

item.title: Australians urged to share their stories as royal commission begins after Bondi attack
item.description: Australians are being encouraged to submit their experiences to assist the royal commission into antisemitism.

item.title: Russia-Ukraine peace talks continue, but will they ever conclude?
item.description: As Ukraine enters its fifth year of war, another round of talks aimed at ending the war in Ukraine could be held at the end of this week. Ukraine, Russia and the United States have held several rounds of talks in Abu Dhabi and Geneva; as Washington seeks an end to four years of war since Moscow's 2022 full-scale invasion. But how close is a real peace deal?

item.title: UK government updates rules for dual citizens under new passport laws
Severe storms sweep Victoria, leaving thousands without power as 'danger' warnings continue
item.description: Wild storms swept across Victoria on Tuesday night, leaving thousands without power and the SES responding to hundreds of emergency calls.

item.title: Severe storms sweep Victoria, leaving thousands without power as 'danger' warnings continue
item.description: Wild storms swept across Victoria on Tuesday night, leaving thousands without power and the SES responding to hundreds of emergency calls.

item.title: Capital gains tax concessions slammed as unfair and cruel to younger generations
item.description: A Senate inquiry into housing affordability has heard urgent calls for tax reform, with advocates warning that current capital gains concessions favour wealthy investors while locking the younger generation out of the property market. The Australian Council of Trade Unions argues that reducing these tax breaks could boost home ownership by four per cent. The Grattan Institute maintains the primary benefit would be a $6.5 billion budget windfall to fund public housing and support struggling renters.

item.title: Trump's tariffs spark fresh round of market uncertainty
item.description: SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with David Scutt from StoneX Group Inc. about the day's market action, including how Trump's tariffs are causing a fresh wave of uncertaintly, plus why investors are keeping a close eye on the private credit markets

item.title: Police believe remains may belong to missing grandfather | SBS News in Easy English 24 February 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Politicians urged to tackle 'monopoly-like' issue with iPhone payments
item.description: Banks and fintech representatives are accusing tech platforms such as Apple of free-riding amid the rapid growth of digital payments.

item.title: BAFTAs scandal: What is Tourette syndrome, what are tics, and why are people angry?
item.description: Tourette syndrome activist John Davidson says he left the BAFTAs ceremony "aware of the distress my tics were causing".

item.title: $831 billion rebuild cost reveal as Ukrainians in Australia stuck on 'hope and heartbreak'
item.description: As the Russia-Ukraine war enters its fifth year, Ukrainians are exhausted by protracted peace talks and ongoing violence.

item.title: How much you need for a comfortable retirement & AI fears continues to spook investors
item.description: SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Mike Jenneke from UBS Wealth Management about the day's market action including investors’ renewed AI fears; plus Stephanie Youssef talks with Jame Koval from ASFA to find out how much you need for a comfortable retirement.

item.title: Australian jailed in Syria now feared lost in Iraqi prison system
item.description: A Sydney doctor leading a bid to return IS-linked families said Australia has "failed" Yusuf Zahab.

item.title: China responds after banned dance troupe linked to threat that forced PM's evacuation
item.description: The threat referenced Shen Yun — a cultural show linked to the spiritual movement Falun Gong, both of which have been banned by China.

item.title: Can Australia stop a citizen's return? The legal situation facing IS-linked cohort in Syria
item.description: The government wants to block the return of 34 IS-linked women and children to Australia. This is what the law says.

item.title: I've been goth for almost 40 years. Here's what I learned about fitting in
item.description: Emma Baird entered the Perth goth scene in the late 1980s. She believes it was pivotal in relieving her feeling of social isolation.

item.title: Are you on track to retire comfortably? Australia's new super benchmark released
item.description: Despite the increased benchmark, a superannuation expert says one group of Australians are right on track for a comfortable retirement.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Dr Jamal Rifi on efforts to being home Austalian women in an IS group detention camp
item.description: Dr Jamal Rifi is an Australian doctor who has travelled to Syria to try and bring home a group of Australian women and children being held in an IS-group detention camp. The federal government has been adamant it will not help with the group's repatriation, and Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has said any of those who do return will face charges if they are found to have broken Australian law. Dr Rifi has with him passports for the cohort of women and children which have been issued by Australian authorities. He also has another passport in his possession-- for an Australian man Yusuf Zahab, who was thought to be dead, until the SBS Dateline program revealed in 2024 he was alive and in a Syrian prison. Dr Jamal Rifi spoke with SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson from an undisclosed location.

item.title: Full steam ahead: Federal Government launches plans for first high-speed Sydney to Newcastle train service
item.description: The Federal Government has moved to the next stage of Australia's inaugural high-speed rail project, launching a two-year development phase for the Sydney-to-Newcastle link. Construction is slated to begin in 2028. While the project faces a nearly $90 billion price tag and public scepticism over its long history of delays, proponents argue the 180-kilometre network will eventually transform regional housing and productivity across the entire East Coast.

item.title: The UK's new entry rules are here. What do they mean for you?
item.description: UK citizens will need a valid British or Irish passport to enter the country. The decision has spelled chaos for Australian dual citizens.

item.title: The UK's new entry rules are here. What do they mean for you?
item.description: UK citizens will need a valid British or Irish passport to enter the country. The decision has spelled chaos for Australian dual citizens.

item.title: Allies reinforce support for Ukraine on fourth anniversary of Russian invasion
item.description: As Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine enters its fifth year, international pressure is intensifying to end the conflict. The European Union is finalising its 20th sanctions package against Russia, while the United Nations General Assembly has overwhelmingly backed a resolution supporting Ukraine’s territorial integrity.

item.title: SBS News In Easy English 25 February 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: AI blamed for mass Wisetech job cuts & Woolies CEO on reputation rebuild
item.description: Hear from Wisetech CEO Zubin Appoo as he explains why 2000 jobs will go at the company as AI hits its workforce; SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Francesco De Stradis from Ord Minnett to find out the implications for the broader market; plus Woolworths CEO Amanda Bardwell on how the supermarket operator is winning back consumer trust.

item.title: From immigration to Iran: The key moments from Trump's State of the Union address
item.description: The US president has delivered his annual national update, using the opportunity to burnish his achievements during a difficult period.

item.title: Poet Evelyn Araluen wins Victoria's highest literary award for 'uncompromising' collection
item.description: The Goorie/Koori poet has won the $100,000 Victorian Prize for Literature for her poetry collection The Rot.

item.title: Poet Evelyn Araluen wins Victoria's highest literary award for 'uncompromising' collection
item.description: The Goorie/Koori poet has won the $100,000 Victorian Prize for Literature for her poetry collection The Rot.

item.title: Syrian Kurdish official fears IS-linked cohort will 'become radicals' if not taken in
item.description: An official overseeing a Syrian detention camp says Australia must take responsibility for the 34 women and children living there.

item.title: Want your property 'pinch me' moment? Here's how long it will take
item.description: The time it takes to save a deposit to buy a home has blown out, according to a new report.

item.title: Killed, jailed, exiled: How Putin crushes his opponents from within
item.description: Experts say the Kremlin's campaign to quash opposition has intensified, with many Russian activists either jailed or killed.

item.title: Israel responsible for two-thirds of record 129 journalist deaths in 2025, watchdog finds
item.description: Most journalist deaths last year were from armed conflict, making it the second consecutive record-breaking year for fatalities.

item.title: New website and app to safeguard international students in Australia
item.description: For international students studying in Australia, accessing healthcare is often seen as too expensive - or too confusing to navigate. But a new initiative - designed by international students FOR international students - aims to make services easier to find, while breaking down taboos around sexual and mental health.

item.title: 'Dial down the rhetoric' PM says, amid jump in threats to MPs
item.description: The emergency evacuation of the Prime Minister from his Canberra home due to a targeted bomb threat has prompted warnings of a heightened security environment, where online hostility is increasingly manifesting as high-harm physical threats. It follows comments by the Federal Police Commissioner late last year, noting a trend of escalating violence against public officials.

item.title: South African men allegedly lured to fight for Russia in Ukraine return home
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa thanked his Russian counterpart, Vladimir Putin, for helping to secure the group's return.

item.title: What Aussies should do after $450 energy bill 'shock absorber' axed
item.description: The first quarterly energy bills of the year are arriving — and you might notice something is missing.

item.title: Albanese addresses 'difficult' Grace Tame comment as Tame compares him to Morrison
item.description: The prime minister said his comments were misrepresented following criticism on social media.

item.title: Why 90 per cent of Australians will receive the same emergency alert in July
item.description: A long-awaited system will come online this year, with most Australians set to receive identical messages.

item.title: The world watches - and waits - on the eve of US and Iran nuclear talks
item.description: As the United States amasses more warships and aircraft in the Middle East than the world has seen in decades, the possibility of a regional war appears ever-more threatening. There are fears the dispute between the two nations could flare up into a far-reaching conflict, on the eve of a third round of talks between the United States and Iran in Geneva over the Islamic Republic's nuclear program.

item.title: Talks continue between Iran and the US | SBS News in Easy English 26 February 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Syria confirms mass escape of detainees with alleged IS links from al-Hol camp last month
item.description: Syria has confirmed that "more than 138 breaches" have been discovered in the camp's 17km perimeter wall.

item.title: The Chinese dance group at the centre of the Albanese bomb threat — and its link to Trump
item.description: A threat that forced the prime minister to temporarily evacuate his official residence was directed at the Shen Yun company.

item.title: Two patients have died after a fungal outbreak at Sydney hospital
item.description: The outbreak occurred between October and December 2025, and the hospital is currently under redevelopment.

item.title: How the US created a vacuum for the IS-linked cohort trying to return to Australia
item.description: The US is halting support of two IS-linked camps in Syria, saying their help "cannot last forever".

item.title: Ukraine’s grim anniversary, ‘El Mencho’ killed & SOTU, Trump’s very, very long speech
item.description: Can Ukraine hold the line as US support wavers going into a fifth year of fighting Russia’s full-scale invasion? Plus, an explosion of violence in Mexico after the killing of brutal cartel boss ‘El Mencho’ - will calm be restored in time for the World Cup? The US Supreme Court takes down Trump’s tariffs and POTUS uses his high-stakes State of the Union address to accuse Somalis of fraud

item.title: A global spectacle, a local crisis: Cartel violence and Mexico's World Cup moment
item.description: Cartel violence has swept through parts of Mexico in recent days, raising questions over the country's ability to host football's biggest tournament.

item.title: Nvidia results spur AI tech recovery & why most borrowers will cope with rate rise
item.description: Hear from Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang on why he thinks huge capital expenditure on AI technology will continue as SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Heath Moss from HLM Investments about the days market action including a recovery in tech stocks and a look at Qantas' half-year results. Plus Erin Kitson from S&P Global explains how borrowers are likely to cope with rising interest rates.

item.title: 'Tip of the iceberg': Australia faces a rising threat
item.description: In a more polarised world, political threats and violence are on the rise — and Australia is not immune.

item.title: Your guide to the Women's Asian Cup 2026, as the Matildas chase redemption
item.description: The Matildas will be hoping for a better result at the home tournament, after a shock early exit last time around.

item.title: Dateline returns with stories of the people caught in Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: Dateline's new season sees reporter Darren Mara meet ordinary Minnesotans who've found themselves at the heart of US President Donald Trump's crackdown on immigration.

item.title: I run a gay sauna in Australia. You'd be surprised who comes through the door
item.description: Once stigmatised — and illegal — gay saunas like the one Glenn McNamara runs hold a special place in LGBTQI+ history.

item.title: Last-minute apology for 'racist' posts 'doesn't change anything' for NT administrator, Elder says
item.description: The incoming administrator's apology for widely criticised social media posts has not been accepted by some of his critics.

item.title: Clinton says she doesn't recall ever meeting Epstein, has no information about his crimes
item.description: "I do not recall ever encountering Mr Epstein ... I have nothing to add to that," the former first lady said.

item.title: 'Fractured trust': Veteran Navy officer sacked from multicultural board after Israel posts
item.description: Mona Shindy was dumped by Multicultural NSW from its advisory board this week after she shared posts on LinkedIn about Israel and Gaza.

item.title: US democrats vow to have all Epstein files released | Midday Bulletin 27 February 2026
item.description: US democrats vow to have all Epstein files released, after reports key records were knowingly withheld, Key childcare safety reforms come into effect today, across Australia, Australians gather in Las Vegas for the NRL season opener.

item.title: Sussan Ley won't return to parliament for farewell but is 'proud' of leadership milestone
item.description: Becoming the first woman to lead any federal Opposition in Australia was "a milestone for all women to be proud of", Ley said.

item.title: 'Lured in': The Russian recruitment scam sending Africans to fight in Ukraine
item.description: With the Russian Army losing an estimated 30 to 50,000 ((30,000 to 50,000)) soldiers per month, Russia is reportedly using recruitment scams to lure in men from across Africa. Offered enticing salaries and told to sign a contract in Russian, officials say economic incentives and the exploitation of vulnerable people are driving recruitment.

item.title: IRGC terrorism listing welcomed by Kylie Moore-Gilbert
item.description: The Australian government is reviewing its formal designation of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organisation, following the expulsion of Iran's ambassador over allegations of foreign interference and state-sponsored violence. Testimony from experts and former detainees highlights a catastrophic human rights situation, including systematic repression of universities and a nationwide death toll reported to be in the tens of thousands.

item.title: Hillary Clinton testifies in Epstein investigation as Democrats claim cover up
item.description: Political pressure is intensifying over the release of Jeffrey Epstein investigation files as Hillary Clinton appears before U-S leaders, and Democrats accuse the Justice Department of withholding key records.

item.title: New measures to protect children in childcare | SBS News in Easy English 27 February 2025
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: 'Significant progress' made during US and Iran talks — but no sign of a deal yet
item.description: The discussions about the decades-long dispute over Iran's nuclear work come amid growing tension in the Middle East.

item.title: Magda Szubanski says she's in remission after completing chemotherapy for cancer
item.description: The comedian said she was "eternally grateful" to her medical team and those who've wished her well over the past nine months.

item.title: A troubling new record as Australians' financial woes surge
item.description: Last year, a record 111,373 Australians lodged complaints with the financial watchdog — a 14 per cent rise from 2024.

item.title: Pakistan says it's in 'open war' with Afghanistan after launching airstrikes in multiple cities
item.description: It comes after months of clashes between the two neighbours along the frontier.

item.title: Calls for better support for LGBTIQ+ community on substance abuse issues
item.description: Mardi Gras is an occasion for celebration - a time to party for LGBTIQ+ communities. But Mardi Gras events can also invite risky behaviours. There are higher rates of substance use in LGBTIQ+ communities compared to the general population, coupled with a reluctance to seek help from healthcare professionals. Experts are calling for stronger investment in culturally safe care to improve access to appropriate support.

item.title: Man charged with preparing for a terrorist act in Western Australia
item.description: Police allege the 20-year-old planned a "mass casualty event" at public buildings in Perth, including Muslim places of worship.

item.title: AI-related job cuts continues as Block slashes 4,000 roles & Australia Post's digital transformation
item.description: Hear from Block CEO Jack Dorsey as he explains why the company is cutting nearly half of its workforce; SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Omkar Joshi from Opal Capital Management about the future of AI and the impact on stocks. Plus Australia Post CEO Paul Graham details the group's digital transformation plan as it records a fall in profit.

item.title: The 'ISIS bride' who was rendered stateless and couldn't return home
item.description: In recent years, Shamima Begum said she lived in a dusty tent, unable to leave, with an "indefinitely" similar future ahead of her.

item.title: Ali's friend died in Iran awaiting a visa. A new pathway offers hope to other LGBTQI+ refugees
item.description: With resettlement options collapsing worldwide, a new community-led initiative will help LGBTQI+ people get to safety in Australia.

item.title: The detectors on guard 15 years after New Zealand's devastating earthquake in Christchurch
item.description: Fifteen years after a devastating earthquake in New Zealand left 185 people dead, scientists are on guard, ready to spot an impending disaster. In the control rooms at Earth Sciences New Zealand headquarters, seismologists are continuously analysing data from a network of 700 detectors looking for warning signs of an imminent quake.

item.title: Many fleeing war are haunted by painful memories. This Australian hotline takes their calls for help
item.description: A hotline offering multi-lingual support for refugees who’ve fled war, violence and persecution has expanded nationwide. As global conflicts grind on, experts say demand is soaring. A warning: the following story contains details that may distress some listeners.

item.title: Many fleeing war are haunted by painful memories. This Australian hotline takes their calls for help
item.description: A hotline offering multi-lingual support for refugees who’ve fled war, violence and persecution has expanded nationwide. As global conflicts grind on, experts say demand is soaring. A warning: the following story contains details that may distress some listeners.

item.title: Queen Mary to visit Uluru and her Tasmanian hometown during state visit next month
item.description: Queen Mary of Denmark is bringing a large green energy business delegation with her when she and King Frederik visit next month.

item.title: Trump raises prospect of 'friendly takeover' of Cuba
item.description: US President Trump said he had been hearing about the country's woes since he was a little boy.

item.title: WA police foil alleged mass-casualty terror plot, premier says | Midday News Bulletin 28 February 2026
item.description: West Australia's Premier says there is no ongoing threat to safety after a terror plot arrest; Calls for civilians to be protected in hostilities between Pakistan and Afghanistan; and in women's cricket, Australia beat India by five wickets.

item.title: Taliban says it's open to talks after Pakistan bombs Kabul and other Afghan towns
item.description: As the hostilities between Pakistan and Afghanistan are increasing, the Taliban officials say they "want to resolve this matter through dialogue".

item.title: 'Sometimes you have to use force': Trump on Iran
item.description: There is reportedly recognition inside the US camp that taking on Iran would be more difficult than the capture of Nicolas Maduro.

item.title: As the threat of war heightens distress, help may be just a phone call away
item.description: A national hotline offers multilingual support for refugees who've fled war and violence. As global conflict continues, demand is soaring.

item.title: Liberals slam decision not to release 2025 election review | Evening News Bulletin 28 February 2026
item.description: The Liberals decline to release the report into their disastrous 2025 federal election campaign; At least 15 people killed in a Bolivia military plane crash; Both New Zealand and France out of the Sydney leg of Sail GP this weekend.

item.title: Albanese to convene security committee as he backs US action against Iran
item.description: The prime minister lashed the Iranian regime and said it "can never be allowed to develop a nuclear weapon".

item.title: 'We knew it would be worse': Bobbi left Florida, her dog and Trump for a tiny Australian town
item.description: Bobbi Waterman didn't feel safe as a trans woman in the US. In Rupanyup, Victoria, she and her spouse have built a community for themselves.

item.title: Hanad lives in hiding, fearing deportation. This is life during Trump's immigrant crackdown
item.description: In Minnesota, ICE agents have left behind a trail of trauma, with members of the Somali community still living in hiding.

item.title: Iran retaliates, strikes Israel and US bases in the Middle East | Morning News Bulletin 1 March 2026
item.description: Iran launches retaliatory strikes at Israel and US bases in the Middle East; WA's Premier reassures the Muslim community, after a man is charged over a planned terror act; and in sport, the NRL season opener to kick off with a double-header match in Las Vegas.

item.title: The planets align for Ecstatica Galactica at Sydney Mardi Gras 2026
item.description: Thousands have attended this year's Sydney Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras parade, 48 years since the first gay rights parade in Australia in 1978. Australia's biggest celebration of the LGBTIQ-plus community took place days after an announcement that the historic parade route will be added to Australia's National Heritage List.

item.title: INTERVIEW: How to cope with rising power prices
item.description: A new survey by financial comparison website CanStar has found that many Australian households are currently worried or have concerns about future energy bill payments as federal rebates run out this year. This is in addition to an underlying price increase in household electricity bills. Over the last month, they've risen by approximately 4.5% according to the Australian Bureau of Statistics. CanStar's Data Insights Director, Sally Tindall spoke to SBS's Cameron Carr about what the latest data is showing.

item.title: US, Israel launch coordinated attacks on Iran
item.description: The US and Israel have launched an attack on Iran, with the first apparent strike happening near the offices of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Iran has responded with missile strikes across the region. Israel and the US say the objective of the operation is to put an end to the threat from the Ayatollah regime in Iran.

item.title: A new community-led initiative offers support to vulnerable asylum seekers
item.description: Despite record numbers of displaced people worldwide, resettlement options are facing a critical decline - global impacts including shrinking legal pathways, slashing of admission numbers and funding cuts to agencies. But for the first time, a new community-led initiative will support LGBTIQ+ refugees to apply for Australia’s humanitarian program - offering dedicated support for priority processing.

item.title: The fate of Iran's Supreme Leader unclear | Extra Morning News Bulletin 1 March 2026
item.description: The fate of Iran's Supreme Leader unclear, with reports of his assassination; an emergency meeting of the UN Security Council held over Iran; and in football, the Matildas face the Philippines tonight in their opening Women's Asian Cup game.

item.title: Wong warns Australians in Middle East of 'difficult days ahead' as flights cancelled
item.description: Some believe that Middle East airspace could be "shut for quite some time" amid the latest conflict between the US, Israel and Iran.

item.title: Fate of Iran's Supreme Leader unclear, with reports of his assassination | Midday News Bulletin 1 March 2026
item.description: The fate of Iran's Supreme Leader unclear, with reports of his assassination; an emergency meeting of the UN Security Council held over Iran; and in football, the Matildas face the Philippines tonight in their opening Women's Asian Cup game.

item.title: In pictures: Colour, protest and pride at this year's Sydney Mardi Gras
item.description: Chants of both pride and protest echoed around Sydney as thousands of people marched along Oxford Street for the annual Mardi Gras parade.

item.title: In pictures: Colour, protest and pride at this year's Sydney Mardi Gras
item.description: Chants of both pride and protest echoed around Sydney as thousands of people marched along Oxford Street for the annual Mardi Gras parade.

item.title: Iran's Ayatollah Khameini killed in US-Israeli attack; 'He won't be mourned' says Albanese
item.description: .Australian leaders have thrown their support behind the strikes on Iran amid reports Iran's supreme leader has been killed. For the second time in 12 months, the US and Israel have conducted co-ordinated strikes on Iran in what US President Donald Trump called a "massive and ongoing" operation to topple the Iranian administration.

item.title: Iranian state media confirms the death of Iran's supreme leader | Evening News Bulletin 1 March 2026
item.description: Iranian state media confirms the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei; Prime Minister Anthony Albanese reiterates Australia's support for United States military action; And in sport, the situation in the Middle East sending ripple effects across the sporting landscape.

item.title: 'They all had the same request': Strikes hit Fariba's hometown in Iran. Then came the messages
item.description: Iranian-Australians describe a "mixed bag" of emotions after supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed in US-Israeli strikes.

item.title: Were the Israeli-US strikes on Iran legal under international law? These experts say no
item.description: The government's refusal to publicly assess the legality of the strikes risks "trashing" international law, an expert says.

item.title: Delta Goodrem to represent Australia at Eurovision 2026
item.description: Australia will send a huge household name to compete for the 70th Eurovision title in Vienna.

item.title: Delta Goodrem to represent Australia at Eurovision 2026
item.description: Australia will send a huge household name to compete for the 70th Eurovision title in Vienna.

item.title: 'We're grounded': Australian family's regret over passports trapped in 'nightmare' limbo
item.description: Gemma has had to send physical passports, birth certificates and a marriage licence overseas as part of new entry laws.

item.title: Trump says Iran wants talks again after decapitation strike | Morning News Bulletin 2 March 2026
item.description: US President Donald Trump claims Iran wants to resume talks after the decapitation strike on the Ayatollah... a man drowns in flooding in South Australia... and the Matildas defeat the Philippines in the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: Who is leading Iran now — and how will a new supreme leader be chosen?
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead, leaving Iran at war and without its most powerful figure. Here's how the system will choose his successor.

item.title: Will a 'survival pact' in Iran keep the regime alive after Khamenei?
item.description: The death of Iran's supreme leader Ali Khamenei raises serious questions about the future of the regime and its clerical rulers.

item.title: Donald Trump says the US strikes on Iran could last up to four weeks | Midday News Bulletin 2 February 2026
item.description: Donald Trump says the US strikes on Iran could last up to four weeks, Australians stranded in the Middle East as regional air spaces remain closed, Praise for Alyssa Healy as she ends her one-day international career.

item.title: 'People of Iran will decide their fate': Iranian-Australians call for democratic transition
item.description: Iranian-Australians are expressing mixed emotions over the US-Israeli strikes in Iran and the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. While many hope his death will lead to a free Iran, others say they don't believe that Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu have good intentions.

item.title: Trump says the US strikes on Iran could last up to four weeks | SBS News in Easy English 2 March 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Who will rule Iran after Khamenei's assassination?
item.description: Experts are warning regime change in Iran will not be a simple matter, despite the assassination of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in a strike over the weekend. Khamenei's death is far from the end of issues in in the international community surrounding Iran.

item.title: Experts say US Iran attacks broke the law. So what happens now?
item.description: As Iran vows revenge on the United States for toppling its leader and launching attacks that have killed civilians, President Donald Trump is warning there will be more American casualties. But as the United Nations chief condemns the move, there are questions over what legal and diplomatic consequences the US could face for initiating the attacks.

item.title: Australia a tale of two housing markets with the largest gains in unexpected places
item.description: Home values in Sydney and Melbourne have flatlined while mid-sized capitals are posting bumper gains.

item.title: Australia a tale of two housing markets with the largest gains in unexpected places
item.description: Home values in Sydney and Melbourne have flatlined while mid-sized capitals are posting bumper gains.

item.title: When Australians can get the best view of the 'eerie' blood moon
item.description: A rare 'blood moon' will be visible over Australia soon. Here's when to look up.

item.title: Middle East conflict expands to Lebanon | Evening News Bulletin 2 March 2026
item.description: Middle East conflict spreads to Lebanon as Israel and Hezbollah trade blows Opposition welcomes Israel and America's war on Iran Hannah Green becomes third player to win the HSBC Women's World Championship twice.

item.title: $1 a litre more: Three oil price scenarios and how they'd hit Australians
item.description: Australians' wallets will be hit harder the longer the war goes on — and not just at the pump, economists have warned.

item.title: Major parties throw bipartisan support behind US-Israeli offensive against Iran
item.description: There is bipartisan support in Australian politics for a joint US-Israeli offensive dubbed 'Operation Epic Fury', despite warnings of a major breach of international law. While the government backs the action to prevent a nuclear Iran, critics and legal experts question the legality of the strikes and Australia’s potential intelligence role.

item.title: Pauline Hanson censured over remarks there are no 'good Muslims'
item.description: The One Nation leader was criticised after making the inflammatory comments during an interview last month.

item.title: Travel insurance exclusions Australians must know amid flight chaos
item.description: Travellers are being warned war exclusions could limit their cover — even if their itinerary only includes a stopover.

item.title: Oil, gold stocks lift ASX amid Iran conflict; AI job loss fears
item.description: US and Israel’s offensive in Iran has rattled global markets. However, the Australian sharemarket reversed earlier losses, as rising commodity prices saw investors pile into energy and gold stocks, offsetting losses in other sectors. For more on this, Stephanie Youssef spoke with David Walsh, analyst at Sarto Advisory. Plus, a third of Australian workers surveyed think their jobs will disappear in the next five years because of AI. Amelia O'Carrigan, Ranstad Director of Public Sector and Business Support spoke to Stephanie Youssef about this, and the emerging generational divide.

item.title: Delta Goodrem ready to 'eclipse' the Eurovision stage
item.description: One of the country's most commercially successful and globally recognised performers has been chosen to represent Australia at this year's Eurovision song contest in Vienna. Delta Goodrem will take centre-stage at the 70th edition of the contest. It's a milestone year for the competition, and one which has become clouded by controversy.

item.title: Iran's 'weaknesses' have long been clear. So why a major escalation?
item.description: These are the factors that may have influenced the United States and Israel's deadly attack against Iran.

item.title: 'Change has a price': Iranian eyewitnesses speak from the shadows of war
item.description: Messages received by SBS News capture the mood inside Iran as some rejoice over the killing of its supreme leader, while others mourn.

item.title: Death toll from attack in South Sudan rises to 169, including 90 civilians
item.description: An attack by a group of unidentified men has resulted in the loss of 169 lives in South Sudan, a death toll that could rise further.

item.title: 'Reality check': Employers urged to address stubborn gender pay gap
item.description: Stubborn gender pay gaps between men and women in senior levels at work are being highlighted as data from more than 10,000 employers is published.

item.title: Trump won’t rule out ‘boots on the ground’ | Morning News Bulletin March 3 2026
item.description: Donald Trump says he won't rule out putting troops on the ground in Iran; The PM says Australia's ability to provide consular assistance to citizens in Iran is extremely limited; and in sport, Australia's women's football team prepares to face Iran at the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: 'Pushed out of paradise': High migration is often blamed for the housing crisis. Is that right?
item.description: There's a "widely shared" belief that migration is a major contributor to Australia's housing crisis. What does the research say?

item.title: Australia confirms it is not participating in current US war against Iran | Midday News Bulletin 3 March 2026
item.description: Australia's Defence Minister confirms the nation is not a participant in the US-led war against Iran, The government under pressure to return Australians stranded across the Middle East, FIFA world cup bosses face a tough dilemma over Iran's ability to compete in the US based tournament

item.title: 'I don't get bored': Trump warns of prolonged Iran war in first address since strikes
item.description: In his first address since the United States and Israel launched joint strikes on Iran, Donald Trump says his war on Iran could last longer than anticipated. Spreading quickly throughout the region and rocking global financial markets, polls suggest most Americans are already opposed to the strikes.

item.title: INTERVIEW: How legal is the US/Israeli action in Iran?
item.description: As US President Donald Trump warns of a big wave of further attacks on Iran, the UN Security Council is under pressure to act over the US and Israel's strikes. Iran's U-N ambassador, Amir Saeid Iravani says the strikes on the weekend were unlawful - a position shared by many international law experts globally. So what are the facts? Dr Tamer Morris is a senior lecturer at the University of Sydney, and he's speaking with SBS reporter Rayane Tamer, who asked him what international law says about the Israeli/US strikes on Iran.

item.title: Israel strikes in Lebanon turn deadly as Iran conflict widens
item.description: At least 52 people have been killed and more than 150 injured in Lebanon, as Israel targets operations of the Iran-allied militant group Hezbollah, after it launched missiles and drones toward Israel.

item.title: Iran war impacts petrol prices | SBS News in Easy English 3 March 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Australia's new $2,300 visa price increase and more key changes
item.description: Besides doubling graduate visa application fees, the federal government has quietly introduced two other key changes

item.title: US Secretary of State lays out rationale for Iran strikes | Evening News Bulletin 3 March 2026
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio lays out the rationale for pre-emptive strikes on Iran, Police kill an alleged armed intruder in Sydney, The Matildas say their hearts go out to Iran's team ahead of their clash at the Asia Cup.

item.title: Are mosques mourning Ayatollah Khamenei breaking the law?
item.description: Australian leaders are condemning local mourning services for the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, sparking a fierce debate over national security and the limits of free expression.

item.title: Two men with knives shot dead by police in separate incidents in Sydney and Brisbane
item.description: The incidents took place within 40 minutes of each other in Sydney's Potts Point and Tingalpa in Brisbane's east.

item.title: Why some in Australia are mourning Iran's Ayatollah Khamenei, as criticism mounts
item.description: While for many Khamenei was the "embodiment of a repressive regime", one expert explains the religious reasons some are mourning.

item.title: Israeli President Isaac Herzog visited ASIO headquarters during Australian visit
item.description: Australia's intelligence agency has confirmed Herzog met with ASIO director Mike Burgess after senator David Pocock pressed for answers.

item.title: RNF: Gender pay gap still a problem in these high-earning industries
item.description: Across Australia, for every dollar a man earns, women on average are earning 88- point- 8 cents. The government's gender equality agency says the gender pay gap has narrowed to 11. 2 per cent. And while most sectors have seen improvements, pay gaps remain large in some male-dominated industries, such as finance, construction and mining.

item.title: 'Opportunistic': New petrol warning as treasurer orders price gouging scrutiny
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East has sparked fear of petrol price hikes in Australia.

item.title: Middle-east war to impact petrol prices & Life360 CEO on AI developments
item.description: Hear RBA Governor Michele Bullock detail how the Middle-east tensions and hit to oil supply may impact inflation and what it means for interest rates; SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Cameron Gleeson from Betashares to find out what it means for sharemarkets; plus Life360 CEO Lauren Antonoff talks through her company's results and AI future.
